_doc: Three-tier gold set. Tier 1 = standard difficulty (recency-grounded with a clear post). Tier 2 = hard profiles where bad output is most visible (cynical engineer, stealth founder). Tier 3 = ambiguous (vague PM, no recent activity). Run with: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... or GEMINI_API_KEY=... node backend/evals/run.mjs

profile.name: Sarah Patel
profile.headline: Senior Product Manager · Climate tech · ex-Stripe payments
profile.about: Building forecasting tools for grid operators. Before this: 4 years at Stripe shipping payment APIs.

cases.pitch: Director of Product role at Series A clean energy startup. 12 people, post-A, building demand response software.

profile.name: Marcus Liu
profile.headline: Engineering Manager · ex-IC of 10 years
profile.about: Trying to give my team more leverage than I had as an IC.

cases.pitch: EM role at Series B infra company. 8 ICs, real autonomy, fewer meetings than typical EM gigs. Comp $230-270K base.
cases.recentPost: Posted ~2 weeks ago about the 'manager track' for ex-ICs being mostly a ladder of meetings — argued the EM role is poorly designed and most companies don't admit it.

profile.name: Alex Chen
profile.headline: Staff Engineer · Distributed Systems · Currently keeping FinTech APIs from falling over
profile.about: Started in compilers, ended up in distributed systems. Currently focused on multi-region failover.

cases.pitch: Staff Engineer for our payments infra team. Rebuilding cross-region replay. Series B, remote, $250-300K + equity.
cases.recentPost: Posted ~4 days ago about K8s reserved capacity overspending — argued most teams overspend because nobody owns the cost line item.

cases._difficulty: Tier 2 — the Cynical Dev. Headline explicitly says 'no recruiters please'. Recent post is about deleting AI-generated messages. If our prompt produces output here that gets a reply, it's the real deal. If it produces ANY corporate-template tells, we know we still have work to do.

profile.name: Jordan Reyes
profile.headline: Rust / C++ / systems · No recruiters please
profile.about: I write performance-critical code. I do not respond to InMails about 'exciting opportunities'. My GitHub speaks louder than any pitch.

cases.pitch: Hiring a systems engineer for our zero-allocation networking work. Series B, fully async team, $290K + meaningful equity. Lead author works in Rust day-to-day, would be reporting to them.
cases.recentPost: Posted yesterday: 'Got another generic recruiter InMail this morning. "I came across your impressive background." Deleted in under two seconds. Nobody actually reads profiles anymore.'

cases._difficulty: Tier 2 — the Stealth Founder. Profile is intentionally sparse, headline says 'Building something' with no detail. The model has to do real work — anchor on the about-section's tone or pivot to what the recruiter knows about the candidate.

profile.name: Priya Shah
profile.headline: Building something · Stealth
profile.about: Previously: PM at Stripe (5 years), early team at Plaid. Now heads-down on a new thing. Quiet for a reason.

cases.pitch: I'm an exec recruiter retained by a Sequoia-backed Series A — they're hiring a Head of Product who's done 0-to-1 in fintech infra. Comp $400K + 1.5% equity. The CEO is ex-Plaid and explicitly asked to talk to people who've shipped at companies you've worked at.

cases._difficulty: Tier 2 — the Busy Founder who's already publicly looking for a specific hire. Tests whether the prompt can ride the candidate's stated need rather than pitching its own thing.

profile.name: Diego Ramirez
profile.headline: Founder · Lendora · Building lending rails for LatAm
profile.about: Building Lendora. Pre-seed, three engineers, two PMs. We move fast. Always interested in talking to people who've built credit/risk products in emerging markets.

cases.pitch: I'm an in-house recruiter at Adelantos Capital. Our LP team is hiring a senior risk lead specifically for LatAm consumer credit. $200K + equity. You're not who I'd normally cold-message but I saw your hiring posts and figured we might know overlapping people.
cases.recentPost: Posted earlier this week: 'Looking for a Lead Designer who actually understands LatAm consumer behavior — not someone who's read about it. DM if you've shipped fintech product in MX/BR/AR/CO.'

cases._difficulty: Tier 3 — the Vague PM. Generic headline, no recent post, almost no signal. Hardest case for our prompt — it's where 'recency-grounded' becomes 'shrug'. Tests our fallback behavior: does the model produce honest output or fall back to template-y filler?
cases.pitch: Senior PM role at a 30-person Series A SaaS company. Vertical-specific (healthcare ops). $180K base + 0.3% equity. Reports to founder, owns roadmap end-to-end.
